summary: This is a very brief blog post where Joel Spolsky responds to reader questions about his reference to Rick Chapman's upcoming book on stupidity in the software industry. He explains he read the manuscript to write the foreword and promises to announce its release. He also gives a quick shout-out to TopCoder.com as a cool concept for online programming competitions.
key_points: Rick Chapman was writing a book about stupidity in the software industry that Joel found impressive enough to write the foreword for
Joel positions himself self-deprecatingly as 'something of a stupidity maven' to explain why he was asked to write the foreword
Joel highlights TopCoder.com as an interesting new concept for online programming competitions
money_quotes: I just read his manuscript so that I can write the foreword (being something of a stupidity maven, myself).
What a cool idea, online programming competitions!
key_insight: A minor housekeeping post noting an upcoming book on software industry failures and flagging TopCoder as an interesting new platform for competitive programming.
